explanation: Before Solidity 0.5, a function with no visibility specifier silently defaulted to public, so helper functions the author meant to keep internal could be called by anyone. Solidity 0.5+ makes visibility mandatory, but legacy code compiled with older pragmas still carries this trap.
impact: Anyone can call functions that were intended to be internal, such as initialization or fund-moving helpers; the 2017 Parity wallet hack started with an unprotected function that was public by default.
